one_time_description: Settlement income from Treaty Land Entitlement Claim ($252,609,631 via Blueberry River Placeholder Trust) and Implementation Agreement with Province of BC ($55,000,000), plus gain on disposal of capital assets and investment earnings
economic_notes: Nation has investments in Six Nations Ventures L.P. (16.67%), Blueberry River Enterprises LP (100%), 0851799 BC Ltd. dba Creever Trading Post (100%), Blueberry River Resources Ltd. (100%), Bluevest Contractors Inc. (50%), Blueberry Economic Development Inc. (100%), and Investment in Traplines. $55M Implementation Agreement with BC for land restoration and limits on industrial development. May receive up to additional $33M depending on triggering events plus PNG revenue-sharing. Community evacuated May 2023 due to wildfire. $50,000 distributed to each adult member April 3, 2023 from TLE settlement.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED - nation did not prepare a budget as required by public sector accounting standards. Massive one-time settlement income of $307.8M dominates the year: $252.6M Treaty Land Entitlement claim settlement (held in Blueberry River Placeholder Trust) and $55M Implementation Agreement with Province of BC. Cash held in trust of $248.6M is a new balance sheet item. Accumulated surplus jumped from $36.3M to $348.1M. The $50,000 per adult member distribution was paid April 3, 2023 (subsequent event). Community evacuation due to wildfire in May 2023 with costs expected to be substantially recovered from ISC. ISC Treaty Land Entitlement debt largely repaid during year ($4.05M). Province BC not AB - financial statements are for a BC First Nation. No remuneration schedule was included in the extracted text.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
